subject: Cloud Computing
title: Docker: Containers Change Everything
description: A micro-lesson that makes Docker: Containers Change Everything usable.

hook.text: In 2013, a startup showed technology that could package any application into a portable box.

buildup.text: Docker containers were lighter than virtual machines. They started in seconds, not minutes.

discovery.text: A container bundles your code and dependencies together. It runs the same everywhere.

twist.text: Docker delighted developers but created chaos — thousands of containers to track and manage.

climax.text: Docker didn't replace virtual machines. It created a new layer on top of them.

punchline.text: Containers are tiny, portable, and everywhere now.

quiz.question: How do Docker containers differ from virtual machines?
options: Containers are lighter and start in seconds instead of minutes
Containers require dedicated physical hardware to run
Containers can only run one application at a time globally
